title: smilax ovatorotunda f. sadoensis
intro: Smilax ovatorotunda f. sadoensis, also known as the Japanese smilax or sado smilax, is a type of climbing vine native to the Japanese island of Sado. It belongs to the Smilacaceae family and is traditionally used in Japanese herbal medicine for its medicinal properties. The plant is valued for its antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antiviral properties, which are believed to offer various health benefits, including improved immune function, reduced stress and anxiety, and enhanced cardiovascular health. Smilax ovatorotunda f. sadoensis is typically prepared as a decoction or infusion, and is also used in modern herbal supplements and teas to leverage its medicinal properties.
